threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Overground' instead of 'that orange tube,' and don't yet know the fun coffee spots aren't on the main roads.
Saw a guy on the 59 last night arguing passionately with a lamp post about the best kebab shop in Brixton. It was strangely compelling.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found myself sharing a kebab with a stray cat on Weserstraße at 2 AM, realizing this city’s chaos felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you grab a currywurst at 3 AM from a stand lit by fairy lights, right next to a rave still going strong in an abandoned factory.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by their disdain for the new taco fusion spots—they still crave the old school taquerias with a line out the door.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tux on the 21, talking to himself about the best way to win a game of chess. Just another Tuesday in Hayes Valley.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the best coffee spots are in the busy squares, but it’s the hidden corners that brew true magic, like that tiny place on Carrer de les Verdi.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You'll catch them staring blankly at the metro map, lost even on L2, while the locals swipe through their phones, knowing the shortcuts.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus arrives at a random stop and everyone dashes off like it’s a race. Welcome to Hackney!
Saw a guy on the 35 last night arguing with a bin about whether it was a good friend. Bin didn’t respond. London transport at its best.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when the kebab shop owner started asking about my family, remembering my usual order without me saying a word.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can grab a currywurst from a stand while a techno DJ spins right next to you. Only in Berlin do late-night snacks come with a side of beats.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local knows you can’t count on Muni to get you anywhere on time, but you'll still miss the 49 if you run to catch it.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 bus this morning, and it stopped to let a guy off just outside a taco truck—he got out with a full plate before anyone could blink.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always skip the little artisan bakeries; they think all bread is from the big chains. Miss the real local flavor that's just around the corner.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them easily; they still ask for ketchup with their patates braves at Carrer de Torrent de l'Olla.
